meta.source: Corpus de 4 10-K (Lennar FY2024/FY2023, D.R. Horton FY2024/FY2023), chunks locales en data/index/<doc_id>.json. Las preguntas 1-12 refieren a lennar-fy2024.
meta.method: Preguntas diseñadas por Iker Pindter; respuestas extraídas manualmente de los documentos (sin LLM). Los campos source_passage, source_chunk y source_doc son para verificación humana; el harness los ignora. En las cross-company (18-20) son arrays.
meta.note_fiscal_years: Los cierres fiscales difieren: Lennar 30-nov, D.R. Horton 30-sep; 'FY2024' de cada empresa no cubre el mismo periodo calendario.
meta.note_rewording: q4 y q7 se reformularon (2026-07-22) para nombrar a Lennar explícitamente: al expandir el corpus a 4 documentos quedaron ambiguas (no nombraban empresa) y el query analysis las trataba, con razón, como multi-empresa. Las references no cambiaron (ya eran de Lennar y estaban verificadas). El caso multi-empresa lo cubren las preguntas 18-20 a propósito.
meta.status: 1-12 verificadas; 13-20 pendientes de verificación por el experto

questions.question: What were Lennar's total revenues in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: Lennar's total revenues in fiscal year 2024 (ended November 30, 2024) were $35,441,452 thousand, approximately $35.4 billion.

questions.question: What was the dollar value of Lennar's homebuilding backlog at November 30, 2024?
questions.reference: The dollar value of Lennar's backlog at November 30, 2024 was $5,372,784 thousand (approximately $5.4 billion), corresponding to 11,633 homes, with an average sales price of $462,000.
questions.source_passage: Backlog: | At November 30, | Homes | Dollar Value (In thousands) | Average Sales Price | 2024 | 2023 [...] Total | 11,633 | 14,892 | $ | 5,372,784 | 6,633,750 | $ | 462,000 | 445,000

questions.question: How many homes did Lennar deliver in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: Lennar delivered 80,210 new homes in fiscal 2024, including deliveries from unconsolidated entities.
questions.source_passage: New home deliveries, including deliveries from unconsolidated entities, were 80,210 in fiscal 2024, compared to 73,087 in fiscal 2023 and 66,399 in fiscal 2022.

questions.question: What was Lennar's average sales price of homes delivered in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: The average sales price of homes delivered was $423,000 in the year ended November 30, 2024, compared to $446,000 in the year ended November 30, 2023.
questions.source_passage: The average sales price of homes delivered was $423,000 in the year ended November 30, 2024, compared to $446,000 in the year ended November 30, 2023. The decrease [...] was primarily due to pricing to market through an increased use of incentives and product mix.

questions.question: What was Lennar's net earnings in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: Net earnings attributable to Lennar were $3,932,533 thousand (approximately $3.9 billion) in fiscal 2024, or $14.31 per diluted share.
questions.source_passage: Net earnings (including net earnings attributable to noncontrolling interests) | 3,967,655 | 3,961,291 | 4,648,501 | Less: Net earnings attributable to noncontrolling interests | 35,122 | 22,780 | 34,376 | Net earnings attributable to Lennar | $ | 3,932,533 | 3,938,511 | 4,614,125

questions.question: How did Lennar's total revenues in fiscal 2024 compare to fiscal 2023?
questions.reference: Total revenues increased to $35,441,452 thousand in fiscal 2024 from $34,233,366 thousand in fiscal 2023, an increase of approximately $1.2 billion (about 3.5%).

questions.question: How did Lennar's home deliveries in fiscal 2024 compare to fiscal 2023?
questions.reference: New home deliveries increased about 10%, to 80,210 homes in the year ended November 30, 2024 from 73,087 homes in the year ended November 30, 2023.
questions.source_passage: were higher primarily due to a 10% increase in the number of home deliveries, partially offset by a 5% decrease in the average sales price of homes delivered. New home deliveries increased to 80,210 homes in the year ended November 30, 2024 from 73,087 homes in the year ended November 30, 2023.

questions.question: What is Lennar's land-light strategy?
questions.reference: Lennar's land light strategy consists of reducing its years' supply of owned homesites and increasing the percentage of land it controls through options or agreements (including agreements with strategic land banks and joint ventures) rather than ownership. In connection with this strategy, Lennar expects to spin off a significant portion of its land assets to Millrose.
questions.source_passage: Land light strategy - We are focused on reducing our years' supply of owned homesites and increasing the percentage of land we control through options or agreements, including agreements with strategic land banks and joint ventures, rather than ownership. In connection with this strategy, we expect to spin off a significant portion of our land assets to Millrose

questions.question: What are the main risk factors Lennar identifies related to the housing market?
questions.reference: Lennar identifies as a main housing-market risk that the market for new homes is cyclical and a continuing downturn in the homebuilding market could adversely affect its operations: the residential homebuilding industry is sensitive to changes in economic conditions and other factors such as the level of employment, consumer confidence, consumer income, product affordability, availability of financing, inflation and interest rate levels. A related risk is that if homebuyers are not able to obtain suitable mortgage financing, demand for homes and home sales revenues would be reduced.
questions.source_passage: The market for new homes is cyclical, and a continuing downturn in the homebuilding market could adversely affect our operations. | The residential homebuilding industry is sensitive to changes in economic conditions and other factors, such as the level of employment, consumer confidence, consumer income, product affordability, availability of financing, inflation, and interest rate levels. As a result, over the years, demand for new homes has been cyclical [ver también chunk 26: financiamiento hipotecario de compradores]

questions.question: What percentage of Lennar's homesites were controlled versus owned at the end of fiscal 2024?
questions.reference: At November 30, 2024, 82% of Lennar's total homesites were controlled and 18% were owned (393,649 controlled homesites and 85,428 owned homesites, of 479,077 total homesites).
questions.source_passage: Controlled Homesites | Owned Homesites | Total Homesites [...] Total homesites | 393,649 | 85,428 | 479,077 | 1.1 | % of total homesites | 82 | % | 18 | % [corrobora chunk 4: 'At November 30, 2024, 82% of our total homesites were controlled through options with land banks, land sellers and joint ventures compared to 76% at November 30, 2023.']

questions.question: What business segments does Lennar report?
questions.reference: Lennar's operating and reportable segments are: Homebuilding East, Homebuilding Central, Homebuilding Texas, Homebuilding West, Financial Services, Multifamily and Lennar Other.
questions.source_passage: the Company performed an assessment of its operating segments in accordance with ASC 280, Segment Reporting, and determined that the following are its operating and reportable segments: | Homebuilding segments: (1) East (2) Central (3) Texas (4) West | (5) Financial Services | (6) Multifamily | (7) Lennar Other

questions.question: What does Lennar's Financial Services segment do?
questions.reference: Lennar's Financial Services segment primarily provides mortgage financing, title and closing services, primarily for buyers of Lennar's homes, as well as property and casualty insurance. The segment also originates and sells into securitizations commercial mortgage loans through its LMF Commercial business.
questions.source_passage: Financial Services Segment | Our Financial Services reportable segment primarily provides mortgage financing, title and closing services primarily for buyers of our homes, as well as property and casualty insurance. The segment also originates and sells into securitizations commercial mortgage loans through its LMF Commercial business.

questions.question: What were D.R. Horton's total revenues in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: D.R. Horton's consolidated revenues in fiscal year 2024 (ended September 30, 2024) were $36,801.4 million, approximately $36.8 billion.
questions.source_passage: CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF OPERATIONS | Year Ended September 30, | 2024 | 2023 | 2022 | (In millions, except per share data) | Revenues | $ | 36,801.4 | $ | 35,460.4 | $ | 33,480.0

questions.question: How many homes did D.R. Horton close in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: D.R. Horton's homebuilding operations closed 89,690 homes in the year ended September 30, 2024, with an average closing price of $378,000.
questions.source_passage: For the year ended September 30, 2024, our homebuilding operations closed 89,690 homes with an average closing price of $378,000.

questions.question: What was D.R. Horton's net income in fiscal year 2024?
questions.reference: Net income attributable to D.R. Horton, Inc. was $4,756.4 million (approximately $4.8 billion) in fiscal 2024, with diluted earnings per share of $14.34.
questions.source_passage: Net income | 4,806.0 | 4,795.2 | 5,895.6 | Net income attributable to noncontrolling interests | 49.6 | 49.5 | 38.1 | Net income attributable to D.R. Horton, Inc. | $ | 4,756.4 | $ | 4,745.7 | $ | 5,857.5 | Basic net income per common share attributable to D.R. Horton, Inc. | $ | 14.44 [diluted $14.34 según chunk 41: 'Diluted net income per common share attributable to D.R. Horton increased 4% to $14.34']

questions.question: How did D.R. Horton's total revenues change from fiscal 2023 to fiscal 2024?
questions.reference: D.R. Horton's consolidated revenues increased 4% in fiscal 2024, to $36.8 billion ($36,801.4 million) from $35.5 billion ($35,460.4 million) in fiscal 2023.
questions.source_passage: Consolidated Results: | • Consolidated revenues increased 4% to $36.8 billion compared to $35.5 billion. [cifras exactas en chunk 71: 36,801.4 vs 35,460.4]

questions.question: What was Lennar's average sales price of homes delivered in fiscal year 2023?
questions.reference: The average sales price of Lennar homes delivered was $446,000 in the year ended November 30, 2023, compared to $480,000 in the year ended November 30, 2022.
questions.source_passage: The average sales price of homes delivered was $446,000 in the year ended November 30, 2023, compared to $480,000 in the year ended November 30, 2022.

questions.question: Compare Lennar's and D.R. Horton's total revenues in their respective fiscal years 2024.
questions.reference: In their respective fiscal years 2024, D.R. Horton's total revenues were higher: $36,801.4 million (~$36.8 billion, fiscal year ended September 30, 2024) versus Lennar's $35,441,452 thousand (~$35.4 billion, fiscal year ended November 30, 2024) — a difference of roughly $1.4 billion. Note the fiscal years cover different periods: Lennar's ends November 30 and D.R. Horton's ends September 30.
questions.source_passage: [lennar-fy2024, chunk 72] Total revenues | 35,441,452 | 34,233,366 | 33,671,010 ||| [dhi-fy2024, chunk 71] Year Ended September 30, | 2024 | ... | Revenues | $ | 36,801.4 | $ | 35,460.4

questions.question: Which company closed more homes in fiscal year 2024: Lennar or D.R. Horton?
questions.reference: D.R. Horton closed more homes: 89,690 homes in its fiscal year ended September 30, 2024, versus Lennar's 80,210 new home deliveries (including unconsolidated entities) in its fiscal year ended November 30, 2024 — about 9,500 more. Note the fiscal years cover different periods (Horton ends September 30, Lennar November 30).
questions.source_passage: [dhi-fy2024, chunk 2] For the year ended September 30, 2024, our homebuilding operations closed 89,690 homes ||| [lennar-fy2024, chunk 3] New home deliveries, including deliveries from unconsolidated entities, were 80,210 in fiscal 2024

questions.question: What was the value of each company's homebuilding backlog at their respective fiscal year-ends in 2024?
questions.reference: At their respective fiscal year-ends in 2024, Lennar's backlog dollar value was $5,372,784 thousand (~$5.4 billion, 11,633 homes, at November 30, 2024), while D.R. Horton's sales order backlog value was $4.8 billion (12,180 homes, at September 30, 2024). Lennar's backlog value was higher even though D.R. Horton had more homes in backlog; the dates differ because Lennar's fiscal year ends November 30 and D.R. Horton's September 30.
questions.source_passage: [lennar-fy2024, chunk 48] Total | 11,633 | 14,892 | $ | 5,372,784 | 6,633,750 ||| [dhi-fy2024, chunk 9] At September 30, 2024, the value of our backlog of sales orders was $4.8 billion (12,180 homes), a decrease of 19% from $5.9 billion (15,197 homes) at September 30, 2023.
